text.title: Laravel Queues: Job Batching
text.summary: Bus::batch patterns for parallel dataset processing: Batchable trait, cancellation guard, then/catch/finally callbacks, and chunk sizing rules.

rule.statement: Add the Batchable trait to any job that participates in a Bus::batch. The first line of handle() must check $this->batch()->cancelled() and return early if true.
rule.rationale: A failed sibling job may cancel the batch. Without the cancellation check, jobs that already started will complete needlessly, wasting resources and potentially producing inconsistent partial state.

rule.statement: For datasets exceeding 10,000 items, dispatch a meta-job that paginates and dispatches sub-jobs — never load all IDs into a single batch payload.
rule.rationale: Queue payloads are limited by the queue driver (Redis: string size limits; SQS: 256KB). Loading all IDs into one Bus::batch creates a payload that may exceed limits or consume excessive Redis memory.

example.note: then() fires only if all jobs succeed. catch() fires on the first failure. finally() always fires. Use finally() for cleanup that must happen regardless of outcome.

text.reference: ExportUsersJob with Batchable trait and Bus::batch dispatch
